title: Define Python TUI UX and screen map
description: Why this exists: the TUI needs a concrete operator workflow before implementation so it does not become a bag of disconnected panels. What needs to be done: define the screen map, navigation keys, status bar fields, connection state model, inbox/thread layout, compose flow, node/status panels, mesh neighbor view, config editor boundaries, log viewer, diagnostics panel, and small-terminal behavior. Acceptance criteria: every MVP screen has purpose, visible fields, empty/error/loading states, and navigation behavior; LilyGO-style constraints from the visual research are reflected; text fits in 80x24 and narrower fallback layouts; unsupported features have clear terminal-native presentation.
notes:
Python TUI UX/screen map completed for MVP implementation. Visual baseline: compact LilyGO T-Deck/Meshtastic-MUI-inspired workflow from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.1, but branded as LICHEN/native LCI and terminal-native. Global layout: every screen has a one-line top status bar and one-line bottom command bar. Top status fields, left to right with truncation: app name/context, connection path BLE/IP/DEMO, connection state DISCONNECTED/CONNECTING/HANDSHAKE/SYNCED/DEGRADED, device short name or IID suffix, battery percent or voltage or --, GNSS/time state none/stale/fix/time, unread count, active target/route. Bottom command bar shows 4-8 context commands; on narrow terminals collapse to short key labels and expose help via ?. Navigation model: single-screen stack with tabs for Dashboard, Chats, Nodes, Mesh, Config, Logs, Diagnostics. Keys: Tab/Shift-Tab or [/] cycle top-level tabs, number keys jump, arrows move, Enter opens/selects, Esc backs/cancels, / filters, ? help overlay, Ctrl+L reconnect/refresh, q asks quit confirmation. LilyGO-inspired shortcuts may be aliases where sensible: Alt+b theme/backlight placeholder, Fn+t equivalent as t target switch, Fn+g equivalent as g location/time panel, Fn+space equivalent as p ping/trace. Connection state model: Disconnected shows transport picker and recent endpoints; Connecting shows endpoint, elapsed time, retry/cancel; Handshake shows hello/version/features and max payload; Synced means hello plus first node_info/mesh_state received; Degraded means transport up but stale node_info/mesh_state or unsupported feature; Error state shows last transport/protocol error and retry actions. MVP screens: 1 Dashboard: purpose boot landing/status overview. Fields: device identity/name/IID suffix, firmware, hardware, uptime, battery, GNSS/time, radio TX/RX/errors/duty, neighbor count, route count, latest log/error. Empty/loading: skeleton rows until hello/node_info/mesh_state; empty mesh says no mesh_state yet; error row links to Diagnostics. Navigation: Enter on sections opens Node Status, Mesh, Logs; r refresh; c connect. 2 Chats list: purpose channel/DM inbox. Fields: target name/IID, type channel/dm/broadcast, unread marker, last message preview, age, delivery/route hint. Empty: no messages yet with compose disabled until target chosen or connected. Error: protocol send unsupported/disconnected banner. Navigation: Enter opens thread, n new target, / filter, d delete/hide local conversation with confirmation. 3 Thread/Compose: purpose read and send messages. Layout: scrollback with remote-left/local-right alignment in terminal columns where width allows; narrow fallback prefixes '<' remote and '>' local. Fields per message: sender/target suffix, age/time, RSSI/SNR/hops when available, delivery state pending/sent/heard/ACK/failed. Compose bar anchored bottom: target label, editable text, byte/line budget, ack toggle, ttl/port advanced popup, validation/error line. Actions: Enter or Ctrl+Enter sends as chosen by UX implementation; Esc/Ctrl+C cancels current edit/back; t target picker; a ack toggle; retry/details/delete via context menu. States: disabled when disconnected/no target/payload too large/unsupported send_message; preserve draft when switching tabs. 4 Nodes: purpose node and neighbor directory. Fields: IID suffix/name, last heard, role if known, RSSI/SNR/LQI, hops, battery/GNSS icons/text, route freshness. Empty/loading: waiting for mesh_state/node_info; no neighbors state distinct from disconnected. Navigation: Enter opens Node Detail, m message selected node, p ping/trace, / filter. 5 Node Detail/Status: purpose inspect self or selected peer. Fields from node_info and mesh_state: IID, name, firmware, hardware, uptime, battery map, GPS summary, radio stats, memory, route next hop/hops/expires, recent messages/logs. Unsupported fields show -- with reason. Navigation: m message, b back, r refresh/request where transport supports. 6 Mesh view: purpose route/neighbor table, not graphical map for MVP. Fields: destination, next hop, hops, route seq, expires, flags default/border/bidirectional, next-hop RSSI, neighbor LQI/RX/TX. Empty/delta: full-state pending, delta applied marker, route removed rows fade/mark expired. Navigation: sort by hops/RSSI/expires, Enter node detail, p trace route. 7 Config: purpose safe basic config editor. Boundaries: MVP exposes read-only snapshot plus editable device_name, radio tx_power/frequency/SF/bandwidth/coding_rate/sync_word, announce_interval/receive_timeout/tx_jitter when device advertises config_get/set. Sensitive network_key write-only is not shown as a value; if implemented later, present as separate guarded action with confirmation and no echo. Fields show current, pending edit, validation range, persist flag, result/error. Empty/loading: config_get pending; unsupported config shows read-only unavailable state. Navigation: Enter edit cell, s save, x discard, r reload. 8 Logs: purpose device log stream. Fields: time/uptime, level, module, message, optional file:line. States: unsubscribed/off by default, subscribing, streaming, rate-limited, disconnected. Navigation: l toggle subscribe, level filter, / text filter, c clear local view. 9 Diagnostics/Tools: purpose operator troubleshooting. Panels: transport stats, hello/capabilities, last errors, packet/log counters, raw message counters, mesh trace/ping placeholders, build/support matrix. Actions: reconnect, export local session log in future, run ping/trace if supported, open raw-frame tools only when device advertises capability. 10 Help overlay: context-sensitive key map and unsupported-feature explanation; exits with Esc/?. Small terminal behavior: target baseline 80x24. At 80x24 show top status, active main pane, bottom commands; avoid side-by-side except optional wide layout. At 60-79 columns abbreviate labels, hide previews before identifiers, use IID suffixes, collapse command words to keys. Below 60 columns use one-column drill-down, no tables wider than screen, stable ellipses for long names, and keep compose/status bars from overlapping content. Unsupported features: show explicit state such as unsupported by device, not built in this firmware, needs hardware validation, or compatibility-mode only; do not use Meshtastic channel/PSK terms for native LCI except in clearly labeled compatibility screens. Test/snapshot implications for later Beads: capture dashboard disconnected/loading/synced, thread with compose disabled/enabled and delivery states, nodes empty/populated, config unsupported/error, logs unsubscribed/streaming, diagnostics error, and narrow 60-column fallback.
Review addendum for acceptance gaps: Diagnostics/Tools states: loading shows transport/protocol probes pending with spinner text and last completed probe; empty/no-capability shows no diagnostic capabilities advertised and keeps reconnect/support-matrix actions visible; error shows failed probe name, last exception/protocol error, retry/reconnect actions, and raw details collapsed behind Enter. If connected but no mesh/node/log data has arrived, Diagnostics must distinguish transport up from data unavailable. Help overlay states: loading is not needed after static keymap load, but if context metadata is unavailable show global keymap plus context unknown; empty means no context-specific commands and still lists global navigation; error means failed to render detailed help and falls back to plain global shortcuts. Per-screen 80x24 row/field budgets and priority: reserve 1 row top status, 1 row bottom commands, 1 row title/filter when needed, leaving roughly 21 content rows at 80x24. Dashboard budget: 6 rows core device/status, 4 radio/battery/GNSS, 4 mesh summary, 3 latest events, 4 spare/empty/error; hide previews/events first below 80 columns, then radio detail, never hide connection/battery/time. Chats list: 18 conversation rows plus title/filter; columns priority unread, target, age, state, preview; hide preview below 80, hide type below 60. Thread: 15-17 message rows plus 3-5 compose rows; message priority direction, sender suffix, text, state, age, RSSI/SNR; hide RF metrics below 80 and timestamps below 60 unless selected. Nodes: 16 rows plus filter/header; priority name/IID, last heard, RSSI/LQI, hops, battery/GNSS; hide battery/GNSS below 80 and RF details below 60. Node Detail: one-column sections with at most 5 identity/status rows, 5 route/radio rows, 4 battery/GNSS rows, 4 recent activity rows; overflow becomes scrollable sections, not side-by-side. Mesh: 16 table rows; priority dest, next hop, hops, expires, flags; hide RSSI/LQI/RX/TX below 80 and flags detail below 60. Config: 12 visible rows plus edit/result area; group radio/timing/identity, show only current group at narrow widths; hide defaults/help text first, never wrap secret/sensitive values. Logs: 17 rows plus filter/status; priority level, module, message; hide file:line and uptime below 80 unless selected. Diagnostics: 6 transport/hello rows, 5 counters, 5 recent errors, 3 tool actions; wide layouts may split, but baseline uses vertical sections and scrolls. Help: one-column key list, 12-18 rows visible, context commands first then global commands; long descriptions truncate with selectable detail. All screens must use stable truncation/ellipsis, no horizontal scrolling in baseline 80x24, and selected-row detail panels for overflow.
State matrix and naming correction after review: use Native config field name tx_jitter_max, not tx_jitter. Every MVP screen has explicit states as follows. Dashboard loading: waiting for hello/node_info/mesh_state; empty: connected but no node_info/mesh_state yet, show connection facts only; error: transport/protocol error banner plus Diagnostics link. Chats loading: messages/conversations loading from local session cache or waiting for first message state; empty: no conversations/messages, show target picker and compose disabled until connected/target selected; error: send_message unsupported, disconnected, or local cache parse failure with retry/clear local view. Thread/Compose loading: opening target and resolving route/capabilities; empty: selected thread has no messages, compose bar visible if send is supported; error: send failure, ACK timeout, route unavailable, payload invalid, or unsupported send_message, shown inline above compose and on affected message. Nodes loading: waiting for mesh_state/node_info; empty: connected but no neighbors/routes known; error: malformed/stale mesh_state or node_info stream interrupted, with retry/reconnect. Node Detail loading: resolving selected IID from node_info/mesh_state; empty: selected node has only IID and no status/route data yet; error: selected node disappeared, stale route expired, or details unavailable due to protocol/transport error. Mesh loading: waiting for first full mesh_state; empty: full state received with no neighbors/routes; error: malformed delta/full-state mismatch, stale state beyond threshold, or mesh_state unsupported. Config loading: config_get pending; empty: device advertises no config keys or config unsupported; error: config_get/config_set failure, validation failure, partial success, or transport error with failed keys visible. Logs loading: subscribing or waiting for first log entry after enabling; empty: stream active but no entries yet or filtered result has no matches; error: log_subscribe rejected, stream rate-limited, disconnected, or malformed log_entry. Diagnostics loading/empty/error and Help loading/empty/error are as recorded in the prior addendum. Implementation must represent all three state families in terminal snapshots/tests for the screens where data can be absent or transport/protocol calls can fail.
Capability/safety addendum after review: Delivery state UX must be capability-gated. If hello/supported message types or feature flags do not prove ACK/result support, the TUI must not promise ACK as a normal state. Use fallback states: draft, queued locally, accepted by device, sent/no-ack-requested, heard/forwarded when message_received correlation or RF evidence exists, ACK unsupported, failed locally, failed by device/protocol, and timed out waiting for optional ACK. ACK-colored green success is only shown when correlated ACK/result support exists; otherwise use neutral accepted/sent styling. Raw diagnostics must be armed and safety-gated. Raw tools show states raw unsupported, raw supported but rx disabled, requires explicit confirmation/PIN or equivalent local guard, armed/streaming, rate-limited, and error. Do not show an empty raw panel as if no packets exist unless raw_rx is actually enabled and streaming; raw TX/RX actions stay behind an advanced diagnostics gate because raw mode is security/regulatory sensitive. Config UX correction: sync_word is not a routine basic edit despite being listed in Native config. Native LICHEN expects sync word 0x34 and is not Meshtastic RF-compatible; until the Native config contract is reconciled with project guidance, show sync_word read-only by default with warning text native LICHEN expected value 0x34, Meshtastic compatibility changes RF semantics. Any edit must be guarded/advanced, require explicit confirmation, show the protocol-mismatch warning, and may be disabled if firmware reports native-only mode. Basic editable config fields are therefore device_name, tx_power/frequency/SF/bandwidth/coding_rate where supported, announce_interval, receive_timeout, and tx_jitter_max; sync_word is advanced guarded.
Superseding protocol-basis correction after review: the Python TUI MUST be designed around the current LCI contract: IPv6 link over BLE/SLIP/serial/IP carrying CoAP resources, not the older CBOR native protocol draft in spec/lichen-native. Treat any earlier mentions in this Bead of hello, node_info, mesh_state, config_get/set, log_subscribe, send_message, raw_tx/raw_rx message types as historical/invalid for implementation unless project-LICHEN-py4c.1.13 later revives or reconciles that draft. Connection state model is therefore: Transport discovery/selection (BLE LCI UUIDs, serial SLIP, IP endpoint, demo), Link establishment (BLE connected/SLIP stream open/IP route available), IPv6 ready (local/link-local addresses and default route known), CoAP discovery (GET /.well-known/core pending/complete), Resource sync (GET /status, /config, /config/radio, /config/identity and Observe registrations for /status and /status/neighbors where advertised), Synced (discovery plus initial required resources received), Degraded (transport up but CoAP timeout/stale Observe/resource unsupported), Error (transport, IPv6, CoAP response, decode, authorization, or OSCORE error). Capability gating comes from Link-Format discovery attributes, CoAP response codes, Observe support, BLE LCI version/capability characteristics, and OSCORE/security policy, not CBOR hello supported-message lists. Screen/resource mapping: Dashboard uses GET/Observe /status plus /config/identity and /config for name/role; Chats/Thread/Compose use the native LCI messaging resources once defined by project-LICHEN-py4c children or a mesh proxy CoAP resource, and until then show messaging unsupported by this firmware/resource map; Nodes uses /status/neighbors Observe and /status/routes GET, with peer status only when resources exist; Node Detail uses /config/identity for self and neighbor/route/status resources for peers where available; Mesh uses /status/neighbors and /status/routes; Config uses GET/PUT /config and /config/radio plus read-only /config/identity; Logs require a CoAP log resource/Observe that is not yet specified, otherwise Logs shows unsupported by current resource map; Diagnostics uses /.well-known/core, CoAP ping/request timing, transport counters, BLE LCI version/capabilities, and CoAP response/decode errors. Raw-frame diagnostics are out of MVP implementation until project-LICHEN-py4c.1.13 defines a coherent LCI/CoAP raw resource and arming model; the TUI may show a disabled advanced placeholder with the reason raw diagnostics contract unresolved. Delivery states are CoAP/resource based: draft, queued locally, request sent, CoAP ACK/response received, accepted/changed, Observe update/heard where applicable, timeout, rejected with CoAP code, unauthorized, unsupported resource, and local encode/decode/transport failure. ACK terminology must refer to CoAP transaction acknowledgment/response unless a future messaging resource defines end-to-end delivery ACK semantics. Config boundary correction under CoAP: /config/radio exposes sync_word in spec/11-lci.md, but UI still treats it as read-only/advanced guarded with native LICHEN expected 0x34 warning until the product policy explicitly permits changing RF compatibility. The P1 protocol mismatch is tracked and this superseding note is authoritative for project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 closure.
CoAP resource naming correction after review: Config UI must use the current LCI resource payload names, not stale native-CBOR names. Authoritative basic fields are /config name and role, /config/radio freq_mhz, bw_khz, sf, cr, tx_power_dbm, and read-only/advanced guarded sync_word, plus read-only /config/identity eui64/pubkey/pubkey_fingerprint/addrs. Do not present announce_interval, receive_timeout, or tx_jitter_max as editable LCI fields unless a future CoAP resource explicitly defines them. Labels may render friendly text such as Frequency MHz or TX power dBm, but request/response models must use LCI names. Messaging correction: authoritative optional messaging resource from spec/11-lci.md is POST /msg for send and GET /msg/inbox with Observe for receive. Chats/Thread/Compose probing order: use /.well-known/core discovery first; if /msg and/or /msg/inbox are advertised, enable messaging against those resources. If absent, show messaging unsupported by this firmware/resource map. The existing Python /messages resource is a legacy/current-code mismatch tracked by project-LICHEN-py4c.1.14; the TUI must not prefer /messages over spec /msg. A temporary developer/demo transport may support /messages only when explicitly labeled legacy/demo and must not be considered the production LCI contract. Delivery states under /msg: draft, local validation error, POST pending, CoAP response 2.01 Created accepted with Location-Path if present, rejected by CoAP code, timeout/transport failure, inbox Observe update/heard, and end-to-end ACK only if a future /msg contract adds it. This correction is authoritative over earlier generic messaging-resource wording.
Final resource-ambiguity correction after review: Messaging path is not fully settled across current specs/code. spec/12-apps.md and README prefer POST/GET /msg/inbox plus /msg/sent and /msg/ack; spec/11-lci.md includes optional POST /msg plus GET /msg/inbox; Python currently has /messages. Therefore Chats/Thread/Compose must be discovery-driven and must not hard-code /msg as authoritative until project-LICHEN-py4c.1.14 resolves the contract. Probing/display rule: if /.well-known/core advertises /msg/inbox with POST/GET or equivalent link attributes, prefer it as the standards-app messaging resource; if only /msg is advertised, enable it as LCI-draft messaging and label capability accordingly; if only /messages is present, enable only in explicitly marked legacy/demo mode; if none are present, show messaging unsupported by current resource map. Delivery/ACK presentation must follow the advertised resource contract: /msg/inbox Created/Location-Path and optional /msg/ack where specified, /msg draft behavior where explicitly advertised, no end-to-end ACK otherwise. Mesh/proxy correction: LCI mesh reachability is also ambiguous: spec/11-lci.md advertises /mesh;rt=proxy but section 17.5.5 says direct IPv6 routing needs no special proxy, and Python exposes /proxy. Until project-LICHEN-py4c.1.15 resolves this, Diagnostics/Tools must show mesh proxy contract unresolved; direct IPv6 CoAP to peer addresses is the preferred conceptual model, while /mesh or /proxy are optional discovered capabilities only. The TUI must not present mesh proxy actions as available unless discovery proves a concrete resource and the UI labels which path is active.
close_reason: Completed Python TUI UX/screen map: screens, navigation, status bar, connection states, per-screen loading/empty/error states, 80x24/narrow budgets, CoAP/LCI resource mapping, unsupported-feature presentation, and final review outcomes recorded. Follow-ups created for stale native draft/raw diagnostics, messaging resource path split, and mesh proxy ambiguity.
